div.head: CO–34. Memorandum of Conversation, by the Director of the Office of West Coast Affairs (Coerr)Source: Department of State, Central Files, 721.11/4–760. Secret. Approved in the Office of the Secretary of State on April 13.

list.head: SUBJECT
list.item: Conversation Between the Secretary and President Lleras of Colombia: Guerilla Problem and Survey Team.

list.head: PARTICIPANTS
list.item: Ambassador Sanz de Santamaría of Colombia

div.p: President Lleras said he had been trying to modernize the Colombian army to deal with the dangerous guerilla problem (see Memorandum, same conversation, “Role of the Military”),Document CO–33. and that he had thought of creating two or three special battalions, but that his military officers were arch-conservative in their outlook and methods.

He said the United States had sent a survey mission that had, in its preliminary report,See telegram 439 from Colombia, March 25, 1960, Document CO–26. “recommended some conventional methods that I don’t think will solve the problem”. He said he would be glad to see the final report.

President Lleras emphasized the Colombian military’s need for rapid transport. To be effective, anti-guerilla forces must arrive at the scene of danger or violence rapidly, not “two days later to pick up the dead”. He had thought of helicopters. Mr. Rubottom said he understood that helio-couriers, with short take-off and heavy carrying capacity, were less vulnerable than helicopters and more suited to the work in Colombia, apart from being far cheaper. He said one had been offered to the Colombian Government on loan, and was waiting orders at Brownsville.

President Lleras agreed with the preliminary report’s emphasis on the need for improving the Colombian Government’s intelligence. He suggested that Colombia might send up some men for training, and he was assured this could be arranged. After another mention by Mr. Rubottom of the availability of the helio-courier plane, the President indicated willingness to have his Government receive it for trial.
